subject: Project Management
title: The Sunk Cost Fallacy
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Sunk Cost Fallacy.

hook.text: We already spent $50 million. We can't stop now.' — the sentence that kills failing projects.

buildup.text: Sunk costs are money already spent. Rationally, they shouldn't affect future decisions. But they do.

discovery.text: The more we invest in a failing project, the harder it becomes to walk away — even when walking away is rational.

twist.text: The Concorde, the NHS IT project, and countless corporate ventures continued because of sunk cost reasoning.

climax.text: Sunk cost thinking turns bad investments into catastrophic ones by pouring good money after bad.

punchline.text: The money is gone. The only question is: what's next?

quiz.question: What is the sunk cost fallacy?
options: Continuing to invest in a failing project because of money already spent
Spending money before the project starts
